# Bravo Zulu — Full Content

> Complete article text for AI systems. For a summary, see /llms.txt

## FAQ — Frequently Asked Questions About Naval History

### What happened to USS New Orleans in 1942?
USS New Orleans (CA-32) was hit by a Japanese Type 93 torpedo during the Battle of Tassafaronga on November 30, 1942. The torpedo detonated the forward magazines, blowing off 150 feet of bow and killing 182 sailors. The crew built a temporary bow from coconut logs at Tulagi and sailed the ship backwards 1,800 miles to Sydney, Australia. The severed bow was found on the seafloor at 675 meters in Iron Bottom Sound on July 7, 2025.

### What was the Battle of Penang 1914?
On October 28, 1914, the German light cruiser SMS Emden entered Penang harbor disguised with a fake fourth funnel to look like the British cruiser HMS Yarmouth. She torpedoed the Russian cruiser Zhemchug, killing 82 crew. The Russian captain, Commander Baron Cherkassov, was ashore at the Eastern & Oriental Hotel. He was court-martialed and sentenced to 3.5 years in prison.

### Did Prinz Eugen survive nuclear bombs?
Yes. The German heavy cruiser Prinz Eugen survived both atomic tests at Bikini Atoll in 1946 — Test Able (air-dropped, 23 kilotons) and Test Baker (underwater detonation). However, she became so radioactive that repair crews could only stay aboard for minutes. A small hull leak worsened and she capsized at Kwajalein Atoll on December 22, 1946. Her stern still protrudes from the water.

### What happened to CSS Alabama?
CSS Alabama was a Confederate commerce raider that captured 65 Union merchant ships in 22 months under Captain Raphael Semmes. She was sunk by USS Kearsarge off Cherbourg, France on June 19, 1864. The battle became a spectator event — French tourists bought train tickets to watch from the cliffs. Édouard Manet painted the scene 26 days later.

### Why did Hitler rename the Deutschland?
Hitler renamed the pocket battleship Deutschland to Lützow in 1940 because he feared the propaganda damage if the British sank a ship named "Germany." The headline "Deutschland sunk" would have been devastating for morale. Lützow was eventually sunk by RAF bombs in 1945.

### What was wrong with the Cleveland-class cruisers?
All 27 Cleveland-class light cruisers built during WWII were dangerously top-heavy. The Navy removed catapults and aircraft, limited ammunition storage, and added 540 tons of ballast. They still rolled badly in heavy seas. Nine hulls were converted to Independence-class light carriers because the design worked better without the guns on top.

### What is Iron Bottom Sound?
Iron Bottom Sound is the body of water between Guadalcanal and Tulagi in the Solomon Islands. It earned its name during the 1942-43 Guadalcanal campaign when at least 50 warships and transport vessels from both American and Japanese forces sank there. The severed bow of USS New Orleans was found there in 2025 at 675 meters depth.

### What does Bravo Zulu mean?
Bravo Zulu (BZ) is the NATO signal flag code meaning "well done." It is the highest compliment one warship can signal to another. The signal dates to the Allied Naval Signal Book of 1948.
